articles.title: HOW TO CREATE AN ENDPOINT FIREWALL STRATEGY
articles.summary: Join ThreatLocker for expert insights on securing corporate devices inside and outside your network.

articles.title: CRITICAL FLAWS IN OLLAMA AI FRAMEWORK COULD ENABLE DOS, MODEL THEFT, AND POISONING
articles.summary: Researchers identified six security vulnerabilities in Ollama, an open-source LLM framework, related to issues like denial-of-service and path traversal. Four have been patched. Users are advised to filter exposed endpoints for the remaining vulnerabilities through proxies or firewalls.

articles.title: CANADA ORDERS TIKTOK TO CLOSE OFFICES OVER ‘SECURITY RISKS'
articles.summary: Canada has ordered TikTok to cease business operations in the country, citing national security risks. Users can still access and post content. The government's decision affects TikTok's Vancouver and Toronto offices but doesn't ban the app. TikTok plans to challenge the order, stating it will destroy hundreds of local jobs.

articles.title: VULNERABILITIES OF REALTEK SD CARD READER DRIVER, PART 1
articles.summary: The Realtek SD card reader driver has multiple vulnerabilities that allow unauthorized access to kernel memory, including leaking kernel memory and writing to arbitrary kernel addresses. These vulnerabilities can be exploited through specific control codes, such as IOCTL_SCSI_PASS_THROUGH_DIRECT. They enable users to read and write physical memory from user mode via the DMA capability of the device. Exploiting these flaws can lead to significant Windows kernel memory exploration and manipulation.

articles.title: BREAKING DOWN MULTIPART PARSERS: FILE UPLOAD VALIDATION BYPASS
articles.summary: Parsers often fail to fully comply with RFC standards, allowing for various ways to bypass file upload validation. Techniques like altering headers, duplicating parameters, and manipulating content types can deceive validation mechanisms. Attackers can slip malicious inputs past security measures by exploiting loopholes in parsers' handling of multipart data.

articles.title: HTTP SECURITY HEADERS: A COMPLETE GUIDE TO HTTP HEADERS
articles.summary: This post defines all the security headers that are essential for protecting web applications from various vulnerabilities, such as Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks. These headers provide critical security measures like controlling cross-origin resource sharing and preventing unauthorized sharing of browsing contexts. Websites can enhance security and ensure a safe browsing experience for users by implementing and configuring these headers properly.

articles.title: EXPLOITING A BLIND FORMAT STRING VULNERABILITY IN MODERN BINARIES: A CASE STUDY FROM PWN2OWN IRELAND 2024
articles.summary: This post explains how an exploit was developed to gain write access to the stack by manipulating a looping pointer and creating a double stack pointer. The exploit involved constructing a Return-Oriented Programming (ROP) chain within the stack frame of a vulnerable function using format string techniques. The final steps of the exploit involved adjusting the stack pointer to execute the prepared ROP chain, ultimately leading to the successful exploitation of the blind format string vulnerability.

articles.title: SYMBIOTIC SECURITY (PRODUCT LAUNCH)
articles.summary: Symbiotic integrates vulnerability detection and remediation capabilities directly into the IDE. The platform also provides just-in-time training to developers so that they have the information on how to write secure code.

articles.title: TWYN
articles.summary: Twyn is a security tool that compares the names of dependencies against a set of the most popular ones to determine if there are any similarities between them, preventing users from using a potentially illegitimate one.

articles.title: SASTSWEEP
articles.summary: Sastsweep is a tool designed for identifying vulnerabilities in open source codebases at scale. It can gather and filter for key metrics on repositories like project size and popularity. Sastsweep automatically detects potential vulnerabilities using Semgrep and provides a streamlined HTML report.

articles.title: TRUMP PLANS TO DISMANTLE BIDEN AI SAFEGUARDS AFTER VICTORY
articles.summary: Trump could dismantle Biden's 2023 AI Executive Order. This would affect the US AI Safety Institute and reporting requirements. His team has characterized NIST's AI safety standards as an attempt to control speech through "woke" safety requirements.

articles.title: A LOOK AT THE LATEST POST-QUANTUM SIGNATURE STANDARDIZATION CANDIDATES
articles.summary: This post provides a detailed look into fourteen post-quantum signature schemes. ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and Falcon are notable standards for the second round of "signatures on ramp competition" operated by NIST. Falcon faces challenges due to its complex signing process, while HAWK shows promise for faster and simpler signatures. The aim is to improve post-quantum signature schemes for better performance in TLS connections.

articles.title: NOKIA SAYS HACKERS LEAKED THIRD-PARTY APP SOURCE CODE
articles.summary: Nokia investigated a data breach claim and found that leaked source code belonged to a third party, with no impact on company or customer data. The hacker tried to sell the data, which included SSH keys and logins, but Nokia denies any breach affecting its systems or data. The leaked source code was for a third-party app not developed by Nokia. The company will continue to monitor the situation closely.

articles.title: TSA ISSUES PROPOSED CYBER MANDATES FOR PIPELINES, RAIL, AIRLINES
articles.summary: The TSA has issued proposed cyber mandates for pipelines, rail, and airlines to enhance cybersecurity resilience.

articles.title: GERMANY DRAFTS LAW TO PROTECT RESEARCHERS WHO FIND SECURITY FLAWS
articles.summary: Germany is creating a law to shield security researchers who find and report software vulnerabilities.

articles.title: SOUTH KOREA FINED META $15.67M FOR ILLEGALLY COLLECTING AND SHARING FACEBOOK USERS
articles.summary: South Korea fined Meta $15.67M for illegally sharing Facebook users' sensitive data with advertisers.
